title: Israel Has Killed Esmaeil Khatib, Iran’s Intelligence Minister
summary: The Israeli military said on Wednesday that it had killed Esmaeil Khatib in an airstrike. Iran confirmed the killing.

title: Israel Intensifies Strikes on Beirut, Escalating Its Campaign Against Hezbollah
summary: The Israeli military widened its attacks to the districts in the center of the Lebanese capital, destroying buildings, forcing residents to flee and killing at least 10.

title: As Iran War Drags On, Europe Wants to Avoid a New Migration Crisis
summary: A decade ago, a surge in migration to Europe spurred the far-right’s rise. European leaders now fear the Iran war could set off another crisis, and they have taken tentative steps to prepare.

title: The Cost of the A.I. Boom: A Trade Deficit the President Detests
summary: A recent surge of A.I.-related imports has become an impediment to the smaller trade deficit President Trump wants.

title: Cesar Chavez, a Civil Rights Icon, Is Accused of Abusing Girls for Years
summary: An investigation by The New York Times found extensive evidence that the United Farm Workers co-founder groomed and sexually abused girls who worked in the movement.

title: 6 Takeaways From the NYT Investigation Into Allegations Against Cesar Chavez
summary: The revered union leader who campaigned for farm workers’ rights has been accused by women in the movement of years of sexual assault, some involving girls.

title: NYC High School Student Freed After 10 Months in ICE Detention
summary: The arrest of Dylan Lopez Contreras last year was the first reported case of a public school student in the city being taken by federal immigration agents since President Trump returned to office.

title: How TrumpRx Drug Prices Compare With Those in Other Countries
summary: The TrumpRx website claims to offer the best prices for medications. Here’s where Americans still pay more — and much more.

title: Firm That Planned Trump’s Jan. 6 Rally Received No-Bid Contracts
summary: This administration has given the company, staffed by the president’s allies, multimillion-dollar contracts it was guaranteed to win.

title: Stratton, Illinois Senate Primary Winner, Promises a ‘Fight’ With Trump
summary: Ms. Stratton, a Democrat whose viral campaign ad featured voters’ profane views of Mr. Trump, said her campaign’s aggressive messaging was resonating.

title: Pritzker’s Gamble to Become a Kingmaker in Illinois Pays Off
summary: Gov. JB Pritzker invested capital, both political and the more traditional kind, in the Senate race of his lieutenant governor, Juliana Stratton. Her victory could help them both.

title: ‘Their Power Feels Like Mine’: A Dog Sled Racer Says Goodbye to Her Pack
summary: After 20 years of racing, I wanted to take my sled dogs back into the wilderness.

title: Inside Jeffrey Epstein’s Push to Cleanse His Past Online
summary: After he left jail in 2009, Mr. Epstein hired a host of people to make him look better on Google, Wikipedia and many other places on the web.

title: Silicon Valley Musters Behind-the-Scenes Support for Anthropic
summary: Tech companies have been reluctant to directly confront Trump administration officials over their contract feud with the A.I. start-up.

title: Google Sits Pretty as A.I. Rivals Compete for Pentagon Favor
summary: The tech giant has been rebuilding its relationship with the Defense Department and is poised to benefit as it sidesteps competitors’ controversies.

title: Pakistan to Pause Afghanistan Airstrikes for Eid
summary: At least 143 people were killed in a Pakistani airstrike that hit a drug rehabilitation facility in Kabul on Monday, according to a top U.N. official.

title: He Lost a Leg for Russia. Then, He Says, His Country Betrayed Him.
summary: Imprisoned for murder, Aleksandr Abbasov-Derskhan sought a new start in life and freedom by signing up to fight in Ukraine. But he says promised benefits proved illusory.

title: An Abandoned Russian Tanker Is Drifting at Sea. Will Anyone Stop It?
summary: An attack forced its crew to abandon ship in early March. No country has intervened, despite fears of environmental disaster.

title: Despite Moscow’s Threats, Poland Rules to Extradite Archaeologist
summary: Kyiv accuses Alexander Butyagin, a prominent antiquities scholar, of destroying cultural heritage during his excavations in Russia-occupied Crimea. The Kremlin has condemned his arrest.

title: Got an Idea About Who Robbed the Gardner Museum? Get in Line.
summary: Theories abound as to who pulled off the largest art heist in U.S. history. In a new book, the former F.B.I. agent who handled the case dismisses many of them.

title: Joe Kent’s Resignation Letter Is Dangerous Because It’s Half True
summary: Kent’s resignation letter is partly rooted in truth, even if it taps into old antisemitic tropes about occult Jewish control.

title: If You Hate Trump’s Economy, I Have News for You
summary: It’s all a matter of perspective.

title: Late Night Wonders Which Former President Spoke With Trump
summary: In guessing which ex-leader Trump might have discussed Iran with, Jimmy Fallon said “two things seem equally possible: Either Trump’s lying, or Joe Biden doesn’t remember talking to him.”

title: California Heat Wave Makes Winter Feel Like Summer
summary: An unusual heat wave is pushing temperatures in San Francisco into the 80s this week, and Los Angeles hit 98 on Tuesday.

title: Mamdani Just Hired Her. The Job? ‘The Country’s Hardest Problems.’
summary: Erin Dalton, who is starting her job as New York City’s social services chief, laid out her plans to tackle homelessness, benefit cuts and a budget gap in an interview.

title: Turing Award Goes to Inventors of Quantum Cryptography
summary: In the 1980s, Charles Bennett and Gilles Brassard created a new kind of encryption that would be impregnable.
